tiktok.titulo: The most INSANE 10 minute chocolate pudding that’s going to blow your mind and make you go crazy because it’s just so flipping good! 🤤 Full recipe linked in my bio! make it asap and thank me later! 🥹

facebook.texto: Do this if you want to revamp your wardrobe and throw away all your leggings and baggy t-shirts. She crushed the stubborn fat

analise_criativa.avatar: Slim woman in casual white top and jeans, filmed in a bright kitchen, holding what appears to be a glass with a gelatin mixture — shown in selfie-style mid-video between preparation shots
analise_criativa.editing: Raw, authentic UGC-style vertical video alternating between close-up hands preparing a recipe (baking soda, liquid, bowl) and a talking head moment; low production value but high relatability
analise_criativa.observation: The 3s and 30s frames show hands preparing something without the person visible, which alone would fail the filter — however the 15s frame clearly shows a real woman as the main character, saving the ad. The keyword 'gelatin trick' is reinforced visually by the preparation sequence.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong scroll-stop potential: the combination of a relatable slim woman in everyday clothes + hands-on recipe prep creates curiosity about 'what is she making and why does it work?' The hook copy about throwing away leggings is emotionally charged and aspirational — worth testing against other hooks.

facebook.texto: If you’ve tried everything to lose weight — cutting carbs, endless cardio, eating less, even injections like Ozempic or Mounjaro — and still ended up back where you started, this is for you. Here’s the truth: It’s not your willpower. It’s not your hunger. It’s the methods that have failed you. Quick fixes destroy your metabolism. Carb-cutting wrecks your energy and sleep. “Eat less, move more” ignores your hormones, your stress, and your real life.

analise_criativa.avatar: Middle-aged to older man (50+), appears in the bottom-right corner of a landing page or VSL pre-frame screen. Casual appearance, grey hair, positioned as a relatable everyman figure rather than a medical authority.
analise_criativa.editing: Static or near-static video that mimics a webpage/landing page layout. All three frames are virtually identical, suggesting the video holds on a VSL pre-frame welcome screen for an extended period. Text-heavy overlay dominates the visual with a headline about weight loss myths. Minimal motion editing — designed to function like an advertorial or native content redirect.
analise_criativa.observation: The video appears to be a screen recording or static hold of a VSL pre-frame page rather than a traditional video ad. All three sampled frames (3s, 15s, 30s) are identical, which is unusual and may indicate the creative is intentionally slow-paced to simulate a page view and qualify intent before clicking. Targets UK market with GB geo and 'natural mounjaro' keyword.
analise_criativa.highlight: Strong curiosity-gap headline: '5 Weight Loss Myths That Trigger Late-Night Cravings, Slow Your Metabolism and Make It Harder to Stay Strong and Independent After 50.' This speaks directly to a 50+ audience with high emotional relevance. The anti-injection, anti-willpower framing in the ad copy is well-aligned with the natural Mounjaro search intent. The 'watch this before you try another diet' hook creates urgency without being salesy.

facebook.texto: I lost 38 pounds on GLP-1. Everyone told me I'd never looked better. My sister was the only one who didn't. She said the one thing nobody else in that room had the nerve to say, and I hated her for it. I stopped speaking to her. She was the only person there who actually saw me. And I made her the villain for it. By the time I understood that, I had almost left it too late. Let me back up.

analise_criativa.avatar: Woman named Sarah Mitchell, appears in a casual outdoor social setting (garden party or gathering), shown in profile picture with another person. The ad image shows a real-life candid photo of a woman at what appears to be a social event holding a drink.
analise_criativa.editing: Static image post mimicking an organic Facebook personal post. No heavy design elements. Uses a personal profile aesthetic to create authenticity. Landing page URL visible (trybloomandbond.com). Feels like a genuine personal story shared publicly.
analise_criativa.observation: Ad uses a deeply emotional narrative hook about weight loss via GLP-1 and a strained sister relationship — a story-driven approach that withholds resolution to drive curiosity. The headline 'Nobody Warned Me About This Part of Ozempic' on the landing page preview adds a fear/intrigue layer. This is not a typical before/after or product push — it mimics editorial storytelling.
analise_criativa.highlight: Extremely strong emotional hook using interpersonal conflict post weight loss. The narrative of 'I lost 38 pounds and my sister was the only one who told me the truth' creates powerful scroll-stopping curiosity. Sister-as-villain-turned-hero arc is a sophisticated DR storytelling technique. High potential for emotional engagement and VSL click-through.

facebook.texto: Psssst…. Anti-Spike isn’t just the best supplement to reduce your spikes and support your fasting glucose levels. It also increases your GLP-1 levels by up to 22% (yes, the same molecule as things like Ozempic act on). It’s an amazing natural combo of plant extracts. Comment your favorite ice cream flavor and I’ll send you the link ❤️ Glucose Goddess

analise_criativa.avatar: Young woman with short dark hair, casual black hoodie, standing in a kitchen or home setting, holding and interacting with ice cream containers throughout the video.
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical format, natural home lighting, minimal editing. Feels like an authentic organic reel or TikTok-style video. No overlays or graphics visible. Very raw and conversational in style.
analise_criativa.observation: The Glucose Goddess brand is recognizable and has an established audience. The use of ice cream as a prop is a clever pattern interrupt — it creates a dissonance between the product benefit (glucose control/weight loss) and indulgence, which drives curiosity. Comment-baiting mechanic ('comment your favorite ice cream flavor') is a strong engagement trigger. Product is referenced in copy but not visually shown explicitly.
analise_criativa.highlight: Ice cream prop creates strong scroll-stop dissonance for a health ad. Comment engagement mechanic increases organic reach. GLP-1 angle with a natural/no-injection positioning is highly relevant to the 'natural ozempic' keyword. Authentic creator style builds trust quickly.

facebook.texto: Psssst…. Anti-Spike isn’t just the best supplement to reduce your spikes and support your fasting glucose levels. It also increases your GLP-1 levels by up to 22% (yes, the same molecule as things like Ozempic act on). It’s an amazing natural combo of plant extracts. Comment your favorite ice cream flavor and I’ll send you the link ❤️ Natural supplement with clinically studied ingredients that reduces glucose spikes by up to 40%. Lower fasting glucose, reduce cravings & boost energy. Science-bac

analise_criativa.avatar: Young woman with short dark hair, casual black hoodie, standing in a kitchen or home setting, holding and interacting with ice cream containers throughout the video. Identical presenter and setting to Ad 4.
analise_criativa.editing: Vertical format, natural home lighting, no heavy post-production. Organic reel or TikTok-style aesthetic. Identical visual execution to Ad 4 — appears to be the same creative with extended or modified ad copy.
analise_criativa.observation: This ad appears to be a duplicate or near-duplicate of Ad 4 from the same page (Glucose Goddess), same country (DE), similar active days, same frames, same core copy. The only visible difference is additional text in the ad copy about clinical studies and glucose spike reduction. Likely an A/B test of copy variations on the same video creative.
analise_criativa.highlight: If this is running alongside Ad 4, it suggests the brand is actively split-testing copy length and claim emphasis on the same video. The added clinical language ('clinically studied', 'reduces glucose spikes by up to 40%') may appeal to a more analytical audience segment. Worth monitoring which copy variant drives better CTR.

repeated_creatives.description: Identical presenter (young woman with short dark hair, black hoodie), identical setting (kitchen), identical prop usage (ice cream containers), and near-identical ad copy text — these are the same original video or a minimal variation/duplicate running across two ad sets

frequent_avatars.type: Relatable everyday woman (casual setting, personal transformation story) — positioned as peer not authority, using UGC or selfie-style formats to build trust through authenticity
Science-adjacent or brand spokesperson woman in home/kitchen setting — explains mechanism with props, bridges credibility and relatability without medical authority framing
Older everyman male (50+, grey hair, casual) — positioned as relatable skeptic who tried everything, appeals to burned-out repeat dieters

facebook_cross_analysis.dominant_mechanism: GLP-1 elevation through natural or supplemental means — framed as the same biological pathway as Ozempic/Mounjaro but accessible, safer, and without injection stigma
facebook_cross_analysis.emerging_angle: Social tension and emotional betrayal as a weight loss hook — Ad 3 uses a sister's uncomfortable truth at a social event to create narrative curiosity and deeply personal relatability, bypassing typical before/after or mechanism-first framing entirely
facebook_cross_analysis.market_signal: UK, US, and DE markets are aggressively testing GLP-1 and Ozempic-adjacent positioning for OTC supplements, combining pharmaceutical desire with natural/safe reassurance, while layering in emotionally charged personal narratives and everyday-person avatars to drive click-through among repeat diet failures
